tech level determines maximum possible width, terrain reduces

huge David Weber fanboy.
Scratch that.
huge Honor Harrington series, well some other works.
Safehold series, which Sign Triumph part... much.
1-Unwarranted Doorstopper Syndrome.
It's, far, nine books long... series that could probably have been pared down maybe medium-sized books.
problem that Weber really wants give readers least quick look every major engagement world-spanning war.
Which leads awfully formulaic "Character viewpoint Battle whose outcome pretty much foregone conclusion."
That's enough... then there's problem
2-Tech-Driven Lack Tension: main conceit series rapid reintroduction various bits pieces technology.
good guys benefit from that, while guys effectively opposing introduction that tech.
Then compound that, main protagonist levels tech available well.
think Weber wanted show entirety conflict detail evolution military tech... which interesting from very worldbuilding/world management perspective, really makes most books series feel same.
Sign Triumph, despite being least point first major series, doesn't buck thread.
There's supposedly military conflict looming most book gets neutered simple intelligence ploy series where guys' spies have been ridiculously ineffective from one.)
There's last series naval battles which just victory good guys' navy (because naval conclusively least three books ago.)
ultimately gets taken down count handful pages minor characters, another application magic tech, logical utterly undramatic series events... which coincidentally doesn't resolve core conflict book, that another series taking place years later written same premise.
So... recommendation, unless have enormous amount free time available, passion nitty-gritty Sail/Early steam naval warfare.
